description: Full 4-turn conversation. Prospect is curious about career options, discusses early-stage ML infra ambitions, and ultimately shares resume.

prospect.name: Alex Chen
prospect.title: Senior Software Engineer
prospect.about: Distributed systems engineer with 6 years at Google and Stripe. Passionate about high-scale infrastructure, event-driven architectures, and developer tooling.

recent_posts.text: Just wrapped up migrating our payment service to a new event-driven architecture. The latency improvements were worth every painful debugging session. Distributed systems are hard but beautiful.
Hot take: the best engineers I've worked with all have a habit of writing things down. Docs, ADRs, post-mortems — it compounds over time.

prospect.outreach_topic: Series A ML infra roles in the portfolio
prospect.notes: Strong distributed systems background. Mentioned open to new roles in a comment 3 weeks ago.

replies.text: Thanks Nova! Yeah I'm always curious what's out there. What kind of companies are you working with?

Honestly I love the scale problems at Stripe but I've been itching to work on something earlier stage. The architecture migration was fun but I miss the 0-to-1 feeling. Been looking at some ML infra teams.

Joining for now — I want to learn the ML side more before starting something. Ideally a Series A company where I can own a big chunk of the infra. Definitely open to hearing about what's in your network.
Hey I would love to meet sometime for a more thorough conversation. I'm available anytime next week.
Sure my email is alex.chen@example.com. Let's schedule a call next week.
